highlight.title: AI Amplifies Your Engineering Culture—For Better or Worse
highlight.summary: Research reveals AI doesn't fix broken engineering practices—it accelerates them. Teams with strong testing, code review, and architectural discipline see 5x productivity gains, while those lacking fundamentals see more bugs and technical debt.

resources.title: Edge Computing's 2026 Breakthrough: AI Moves from Cloud to Your Desktop
resources.why_now: Intel's new Core Ultra processors hit 99 TOPS of AI performance, making local inference finally viable. This isn't just faster computing—it's a complete shift from sending data to the cloud to bringing intelligence where the data lives.

resources.title: How to Learn AI in 2026: The Complete Roadmap
resources.why_now: With AI literacy becoming table stakes for most tech roles, this guide cuts through the noise with two practical paths: power user (no code required) versus builder (technical depth). Includes specific free resources like Hugging Face courses and Kaggle competitions.

resources.title: 2026 Engineering Trends: From Experimentation to ROI
resources.why_now: After two years of AI pilots, engineering leaders face CFO demands for measurable impact. Forrester predicts 25% of planned AI investments will be deferred to 2027, making disciplined engineering practices the competitive advantage.

resources.title: ENR FutureTech 2026: Construction Tech's Massive Shift
resources.why_now: Major construction firms are sending full teams to evaluate AI tools for active projects, not future planning. This represents a fundamental shift from treating construction tech as a 'someday' consideration to immediate deployment needs.

claude_take: The pattern is clear: AI is becoming the great amplifier of existing organizational DNA. Companies with strong engineering fundamentals are seeing compound benefits, while those with weak practices are accelerating toward chaos. The winners in 2026 won't be the teams with the most AI tools—they'll be the ones who built the disciplined culture that makes AI actually useful.
